name: mock.patch wrong module path (bare name vs import path)
description: pytest mock.patch uses a bare module name (e.g. 'store._db_path') instead of the fully-qualified import path ('deep_think_mcp.store._db_path'). The patch silently targets the wrong object; the real code runs unpatched and tests pass while covering nothing.

bad_example.why_bad: patch('store._db_path') patches the module object 'store' which may not be what the test's import resolves to. The production code imports via 'from deep_think_mcp import store' — so the correct target is 'deep_think_mcp.store._db_path'. With the wrong path, patch() silently applies to the wrong reference and the store uses its real db_path during tests.

good_example.why_better: Fully-qualified path matches the actual import path of the production code being tested.

detection.note: Flag any patch() call whose first argument is a bare dotted path not prefixed with the package name.
